settings.notificationsDesc: Receive a daily digest when you have unread messages.

page.rdgHint: Which development goals resonate most with you?

security.title: Security
security.passwordDescription: Update the password used for email/password login. Use at least 12 characters, with upper and lower case letters, a number, and a symbol.
security.mismatch: The new passwords do not match.

archetypes.title: Changemappers Archetypes
archetypes.localpractitionerDesc: Works directly in the local community, on the front lines.
archetypes.networkweaverDesc: Builds connections between individuals, organizations and sectors.
archetypes.globalamplifierDesc: Amplifies the voice of initiatives to a wide audience.
archetypes.resourcemobilizerDesc: Secures the resources needed for projects.
archetypes.systemdisruptorDesc: Points to system flaws and forces new thinking.
archetypes.strategicadvisorDesc: Creates vision and provides professional guidance.
archetypes.myceliumDesc: Quietly builds underground networks, connecting people and resources invisibly.
archetypes.keystoneDesc: A pivotal figure whose presence holds the ecosystem together.
archetypes.pollinatorDesc: Carries ideas and energy across contexts, cross-fertilising movements.
archetypes.prismDesc: Refracts complexity into clarity, making the invisible visible.
archetypes.compostDesc: Transforms what has broken down into fertile ground for new growth.
archetypes.sentinelDesc: Guards boundaries, monitors systems, and sounds the alarm when needed.
archetypes.alchemistDesc: Transforms raw challenges into creative breakthroughs and new forms.
archetypes.canopyDesc: Provides shelter, resources, and conditions for others to thrive beneath.
archetypes.sparkDesc: Ignites action where there was stagnation; catalyses rapid change.
archetypes.echoDesc: Amplifies voices and stories that need to be heard more widely.
archetypes.tideDesc: Creates rhythmic, powerful waves of change that reshape the shoreline.
archetypes.horizonDesc: Holds the long view, guiding collective direction toward a regenerative future.

changemakeLevel.title: Futuremaker level
changemakeLevel.subtitle: Where are you on your futuremaker journey?

phases.systemic: Phase III: Systemic and Global Impact

level0.name: Passive Observer
level0.description: You are completely untouched by social issues. You operate automatically, focusing on immediate survival or personal comfort.

level1.name: Passive Empathizer
level1.description: You are aware of problems (climate, inequality). You feel bad when you see them in the news but haven't yet changed your behavior.

level2.name: Conscious Consumer
level2.description: You begin aligning your personal habits with your values. You recycle, buy fair trade, reduce consumption. You are changing yourself.

level3.name: Vocal Advocate
level3.description: You no longer stay silent. You share information, educate your friends and family. You are willing to be socially uncomfortable for a cause.

level4.name: Local Organizer
level4.description: You move from talking to collective action. You organize a neighborhood cleanup, a workplace committee or volunteer. You bring people together.

level5.name: Solution Innovator
level5.description: You don't wait for existing systems to work — you build a new micro-solution. This could be an app, a social enterprise or a new method. You prototype the future.
level5.mindset: "There must be a better way, and I'll build it."

level6.name: Strategic Scaler
level6.description: You have a proven solution and now you scale it. You lead an organization or movement that replicates success. You manage resources and people.
level6.mindset: "How do we reach the most people effectively?"

level7.name: Systems Thinker
level7.description: You don't treat symptoms, you attack root causes. You analyze policy, economics and incentives. You change laws or corporate structures.
level7.mindset: "We need to fix the machine, not just the product."

level8.name: Paradigm Shifter
level8.description: You work at the level of mindsets and culture. You introduce regenerative thinking — moving beyond sustainability toward restorative systems.
level8.mindset: "We need to shift from extraction to restoration."

level9.name: Global Catalyst
level9.description: You lead change efforts across borders. You unite diverse global coalitions to address humanity's existential challenges.
level9.mindset: "We live on one planet; crises have no borders."

connections.title: Connections
connections.messagePlaceholder: Why would you like to connect?

modal.messagePlaceholder: Write a short message so the other person understands your intention for connecting...

MENTORING.title: Request Mentoring Connection
MENTORING.subtitle: Asymmetric knowledge transfer
rules: Be proactive: You are the leader of this relationship. Come with specific questions.
Respect expertise: Don't expect instant solutions, but new perspectives.
Time boundaries: Respect your mentor's time and follow up on agreed steps.

PEER_LEARNING.title: Peer Learning Connection
PEER_LEARNING.subtitle: Learning and exploring together
rules: Equality: You figure out challenges together — neither of you is the absolute expert.
Regularity: Most effective when you have a shared rhythm (e.g. 30-min weekly check-in).

SUPPORT.title: Supportive Connection
SUPPORT.subtitle: A safe space for working through difficulties
rules: Vulnerability: Dare to be honest about your energy levels.
Consent: Always ask: "Do you have capacity for a harder conversation right now?"
Attention: Expect primarily listening and validation, not immediate problem-solving.

default.title: General Connection Request
default.subtitle: Networking and collaboration
rules: Reciprocity: Strive for mutual value creation.
Brief intro: Write why you'd like to connect.

result.title: Assessment Complete!
result.description: Based on your answers, your current changemaker orientation is:

errors.answerRequired: Please select an answer to continue.
errors.maxSelectable: You can select at most {{count}} options.
errors.maxTotal: You can select at most {{count}} options in total.

q1.text: How closely do you follow sustainability and social issues?
options: I do not really engage with them; I have enough of my own problems.
I sometimes read an article, but I do not go deep.
I stay regularly informed, and the current situation concerns me.
I actively look for solutions and positive examples.

q2.text: How willing are you to change your consumption habits for the environment or society?
options: Not at all; convenience matters most to me.
Yes, if it does not require major sacrifice or extra cost.
I consciously watch what I buy and am willing to pay more for sustainability.
I have fundamentally reorganized my lifestyle around sustainability.

q3.text: How often do you share information or publicly stand up for an important social or ecological cause?
options: Never; I do not like conflict.
I only talk about this with close friends.
I often share articles or petitions and own my opinion publicly.
I organize campaigns, write articles, or give talks myself.

q4.text: How much do you participate in local community life or volunteer work?
options: Not at all; I do not have the time or energy.
Rarely, I join occasional cleanups or charity actions when invited.
I regularly volunteer with an organization or local initiative.
I start and lead local community projects myself.

q5.text: Have you developed your own new solution to a problem?
options: No, I usually follow proven paths.
I introduce smaller innovations in my work or personal life.
Yes, I developed something useful for a smaller community or organization.
I created an innovative model or project with measurable broader impact.

q6.text: When you face a problem, what time horizon do you usually use for solutions?
options: How I can get past the immediate discomfort fastest.
How I can solve it so it will not be a problem for a while.
I look for durable solutions that understand root causes.
I think systemically and focus on changing root structures over decades.

q7.text: How able are you to mobilize and coordinate resources for a goal?
options: I mostly work alone and organizing is not my strength.
I can involve a few acquaintances in smaller things.
I can lead and motivate a team or secure smaller support.
I successfully mobilize larger groups, coordinate organizations, or bring in significant resources.

q8.text: What level is your network-building ability?
options: I do not like meeting new people and mostly talk to people I already know.
I am open to new connections when I meet like-minded people.
I actively build relationships across fields to reach my goals.
My main activity is connecting people, projects, and systems into shared platforms.

q9.text: What do you see as your main personal task or calling?
options: Survive everyday life and care for my family.
Do work that does no harm while enjoying life.
Actively improve my own environment and community.
Help build the foundations of a new sustainable and just social or economic paradigm.

q10.text: How do you relate to international and transnational collaboration?
options: I am only interested in my immediate micro-environment.
I follow international news but am not part of such networks.
I sometimes learn from examples abroad or attend international events.
I actively participate in international networks and exchange knowledge globally.

discoverability.showOnMapDesc: Your nickname may appear on the Discovery map. Anonymous visitors only receive a country-level marker; signed-in users see the precision you choose below.
discoverability.allowConnectionsDesc: Others can send you connection requests

country.label: Country
country.example: Hungary only; marker is placed around the country center.

exact.label: Precise
exact.example: Exact selected place; coordinates only, no address stored.

fediverse.whatThisControlsDesc: These settings decide what appears on your public profile page, what can be exposed through ActivityPub, and whether external profile links should be marked with rel="me" for identity verification.
fediverse.privacyNoteDesc: Changemappers can stop exposing future data immediately, but remote fediverse servers may already have copied public content. Use ActivityPub only for information you are comfortable making broadly public.
fediverse.activitypubBlocked: Your overall profile visibility is currently {{visibility}}. ActivityPub export only works while your profile visibility is PUBLIC.
fediverse.firstConsentNotRecorded: First consent recorded: not yet recorded
fediverse.savedMessage: Fediverse and public sharing settings saved.

showAvatar.label: Show avatar
showAvatar.desc: Allow your profile photo to appear on your public profile page.

showCoverImage.label: Show cover image
showCoverImage.desc: Allow your cover image to appear on your public profile page.

showBio.label: Show bio
showBio.desc: Expose your written introduction on your public profile.

showLocation.label: Show location
showLocation.desc: Show your city and country on your public profile.

showWebsite.label: Show website
showWebsite.desc: Display your website on the public profile page.

showSocialLinks.label: Show social links
showSocialLinks.desc: Display external social/profile links on the public profile page.

showSkills.label: Show skills
showSkills.desc: Expose your general skills on the public profile page.

showValues.label: Show values
showValues.desc: Expose your values on the public profile page.

showInterests.label: Show interests
showInterests.desc: Expose your interests on the public profile page.

showIntentions.label: Show intentions and availability
showIntentions.desc: Expose current intentions, availability, and collaboration preferences on the public profile page.

enableActivityPub.label: Enable ActivityPub
enableActivityPub.desc: Allow Changemappers to expose a machine-readable fediverse actor for your public profile.

discoverableActor.label: Discoverable actor
discoverableActor.desc: Mark your actor as discoverable to compatible fediverse software.

exposeProfile.label: Expose profile through ActivityPub
exposeProfile.desc: Make your public profile available through ActivityPub actor endpoints.

exposePublicPosts.label: Expose public feed posts through ActivityPub
exposePublicPosts.desc: Allow only feed posts already marked PUBLIC to appear in your ActivityPub outbox and note endpoints.

showWebsiteInActor.label: Include website in actor profile
showWebsiteInActor.desc: Expose your website as profile metadata in ActivityPub.

showSocialLinksInActor.label: Include social links in actor profile
showSocialLinksInActor.desc: Expose your external profile links in ActivityPub actor metadata.

showLocationInActor.label: Include location in actor profile
showLocationInActor.desc: Expose your city/country as ActivityPub metadata.

relMeLinks.label: Mark external links with rel="me"
relMeLinks.desc: Useful for Mastodon-style profile verification. Only enable this for links that really belong to you.

showOffers.label: Show offers
showOffers.desc: Expose what you can offer on the public profile page.

showNeeds.label: Show needs
showNeeds.desc: Expose what you are looking for on the public profile page.

showRdgAreas.label: Show RDG areas
showRdgAreas.desc: Expose your regenerative development areas on the public profile page.

showCauses.label: Show causes
showCauses.desc: Expose your main and interested causes on the public profile page.

showArchetypes.label: Show archetypes
showArchetypes.desc: Expose your changemaker archetypes on the public profile page.

showChangemakerLevel.label: Show futuremaker level
showChangemakerLevel.desc: Expose your futuremaker level on the public profile page.

showBoundaries.label: Show boundaries
showBoundaries.desc: Expose capacity boundaries and collaboration limits on the public profile page.

consent.title: First-time fediverse consent
consent.body1: Enabling ActivityPub can make your public profile and public posts available to remote servers outside Changemappers.
consent.body2: Those servers may cache copies, index your public content, and apply their own moderation and retention rules. Turning this off later cannot guarantee immediate removal everywhere.
consent.body3: Only profiles with overall visibility set to PUBLIC can be exposed through ActivityPub. Registered-only or connections-only profiles remain blocked from protocol export.
consent.checkboxLabel: I understand that enabling fediverse sharing can publish my public data beyond this site and may continue to be visible on remote servers after I change settings later.
